 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","title":"EPIC: Cockpit Runtime (ntm + multi-agent session ops)","description":"# Goal
Make multi-agent work *practically runnable* as a repeatable cockpit workflow.

This epic defines and operationalizes the “live” layer of the system:
- how we run Codex CLI / Claude Code / Gemini CLI side-by-side
- how those agents coordinate via Agent Mail threads
- how sessions are named, tracked, and wrapped up without chaos

---

# Why this exists (Brenner-aligned)
Brenner optimized for **evidence per week** and cheap, decisive moves.

A cockpit should:
- reduce context-switching overhead
- make parallel hypotheses cheap
- make it easy to compare representations across models
- keep the group honest (severe audience / third alternative)

---

# Key primitives
- **ntm** (Named Tmux Manager): live parallelism (spawn panes, broadcast prompts, capture output)
- **Agent Mail threads**: durable coordination + audit trail
- **Thread ID join-key**: thread_id ↔ ntm session name ↔ artifact file naming

---

# Non-goals
- Re-implement ntm inside Brenner Bot.
- Build vendor-API “agent orchestration”.

---

# Deliverables
- A documented cockpit runbook (operators can run a session without reading code).
- A session identity contract (thread id conventions; mapping to Beads IDs).
- Optional: a minimal `.ntm/` project template (prompt palette + helper commands) *if* it’s worth committing to repo.

---

# Definition of done
- A new operator can:
  - start a session (create thread id, choose excerpt, send kickoff)
  - run 2–3 agents in parallel in ntm
  - get structured deltas back into the thread
  - compile/publish the canonical artifact
  - perform a clean wrap-up (persist artifacts, update memory optionally)
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.665348793-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.665348793-05:00","labels":["agent-mail","cockpit","ntm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:08.695718585-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.1","title":"Cockpit: session identity contract (thread_id ↔ ntm ↔ artifacts)","description":"# Goal
Extend the protocol kernel’s Agent Mail thread contract into a *cockpit-grade* identity contract.

Canonical thread conventions live in:
- brenner_bot-5so.1.4 (Agent Mail thread contract)
- brenner_bot-5so.1.4.1 (thread + subject conventions)

This item focuses on the extra join-key glue that makes sessions runnable:
- mapping thread_id ↔ ntm session name
- mapping thread_id ↔ persisted artifact paths
- making it obvious how to find “the thing” later

---

# Why this exists
Without a single identity contract, we get:
- scattered conversations
- artifacts that can’t be linked back to provenance
- unusable memory / indexing

---

# Acceptance
- Everyone on the team can answer: “where do I look to find the thread + artifact for this bead?”
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:17.821401698-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:15:49.348136972-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:15:49.348136972-05:00","close_reason":"All P1 children complete","labels":["agent-mail","cockpit","design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T21:03:17.85087335-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
